description: A task retries indefinitely on a deterministic error, hiding a real outage behind constant churn.
body: Failure mode: a task is configured with unlimited or excessive retries and retries a non-transient failure (a schema break or bad credential), so it churns continuously, masks the real breakage, and wastes resources while no alert fires. Detection signals: a task stuck retrying for a long time with the same error, and resource usage from constant restarts without a final-failure notification. Do not raise the retry count further. Safer fallback: bound retries with backoff for transient faults only, fail fast on deterministic errors, wire a final-failure alert with context, and treat retry exhaustion as a real incident to investigate rather than auto-reset.
